evidence: risk_model.py contains 5 training functions (train_risk_model, train_tier1_risk_model, train_design_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_risk_model, train_tier1_v1_design_model) with near-identical boilerplate: fit, predict, compute accuracy/roc_auc/confusion_matrix/classification_report/feature_importances, handle ValueError on ROC AUC. The evaluation block is copy-pasted verbatim across all 5 functions (~30 lines each).

gnn_model.py SatelliteGNN class docstring restates the module docstring's architecture description almost word-for-word. The __init__ docstring restates parameter names (e.g. 'node_features: Dimension of node features') that are already self-documenting from the type annotation and default value. The inline comment '# GCLSTM: Graph Convolutional LSTM' restates the variable name 'self.recurrent = GCLSTM(...)'.

engine.py has pass-through wrappers: get_graph_at_step() and iter_graphs() simply forward to self.adapter with no added logic. Comments like '# Initialize HypatiaAdapter with constellation parameters' and '# Generate TLEs and compute ISLs on startup' restate the code immediately below.

visualize_constellation.py has restating comments throughout: '# Draw ISL links', '# Draw satellites as blue dots', '# Add legend', '# Add equator and prime meridian' all echo the code on the next line without adding insight.

Scripts use formulaic print-based logging with ceremony: step numbering patterns like '[1/4] Initializing...', separator lines '= * 60', and verbose parameter echoing (printing every config field). This pattern appears in export_design_dataset.py, export_failure_dataset.py, failure_sweep.py, train_design_risk_model.py, and visualize_constellation.py.

issues.summary: 5 training functions in risk_model.py share ~30 lines of identical evaluation boilerplate
evidence: train_risk_model (lines 235-283), train_tier1_risk_model (164-225), train_design_risk_model (106-153), train_tier1_v1_risk_model (343-423), and train_tier1_v1_design_model (426-507) all repeat the same pattern: clf.fit, clf.predict, clf.predict_proba, accuracy_score, try/except roc_auc_score, confusion_matrix, classification_report, feature_importances dict construction
The only meaningful differences are: which feature columns to use, which label column, and whether to include extra metadata (label_column, num_samples, positive_rate) in the metrics dict
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared _evaluate_classifier(clf, X_test, y_test, feature_names) -> dict helper that computes all metrics. Each training function calls it after fitting. This reduces ~150 lines of duplicated evaluation code to ~30 lines plus 5 one-line calls.

issues.summary: SatelliteGNN class and __init__ docstrings restate information already in module docstring and type annotations
evidence: Module docstring (lines 2-13) describes Architecture with GCLSTM, Global Mean Pooling, Linear Classifier. Class docstring (lines 29-43) repeats the same architecture list nearly verbatim: '1. GCLSTM (recurrent)...', '2. Global Mean Pool...', '3. Linear...'

__init__ docstring (lines 52-58) restates parameter names: 'node_features: Dimension of node features' when the signature already says 'node_features: int = 3'. The Attributes block in the class docstring also duplicates these.
Inline comment '# GCLSTM: Graph Convolutional LSTM' on line 65 restates what GCLSTM stands for, which is already in the module and class docstrings
issues.suggestion: Keep the module-level docstring as the authoritative architecture description. Reduce the class docstring to one sentence ('GCLSTM-based classifier for temporal satellite network graphs.'). Remove the __init__ docstring since parameters are self-documenting with type annotations and defaults. Remove the inline GCLSTM expansion comment.

issues.summary: Legacy engine has pass-through wrappers that forward directly to self.adapter with no added logic
evidence: get_graph_at_step (line 107-109): 'return self.adapter.get_graph_at_step(time_step)' — pure delegation
iter_graphs (line 111-113): 'return self.adapter.iter_graphs()' — pure delegation
num_steps property (line 116-118): 'return self.adapter.num_steps' — pure delegation
These add no auth, logging, metrics, or caching — they are pure forwarding
issues.suggestion: Since the module is quarantined/deprecated, the minimal fix is to add a note that callers should use HypatiaAdapter directly. If the legacy wrapper must stay, collapse to exposing self.adapter as a public attribute so callers access methods directly rather than through pass-throughs.

issues.summary: visualize_constellation.py has 6+ comments that restate the code without adding insight
evidence: Line 86: '# Draw ISL links' before a for loop iterating over graph.edges
Line 112: '# Draw satellites as blue dots' before ax.scatter(..., c="blue")
Line 115: '# Add legend' before legend_elements construction
Line 82: '# Add equator and prime meridian' before axhline(y=0) and axvline(x=0)
Line 76: '# Draw background grid' before ax.set_facecolor and ax.grid calls
issues.suggestion: Remove comments that restate the code. Keep only comments explaining non-obvious decisions (e.g., why dateline-crossing links are skipped at line 96, which already has a useful comment).

issues.summary: Scripts use ceremonial print-based progress output with step numbering and separator lines
evidence: visualize_constellation.py uses numbered steps '[1/4]...[4/4]', double separator '= * 60' at start and end, and prints each config value individually
export_design_dataset.py prints 7 lines of config values that could be summarized in one line or omitted in favor of the config object repr
All 5 scripts independently implement the same pattern of printing config, running computation, printing results with separators
issues.suggestion: For scripts that are CLI tools, either use Python's logging module with configurable verbosity, or add a brief __repr__/__str__ to the config dataclass and print it once. Remove separator-line ceremony ('=' * 60, step numbering) which is cosmetic noise.

issues.summary: engine.py has comments that restate the code line below them
evidence: Line 66: '# Initialize HypatiaAdapter with constellation parameters' before self.adapter = HypatiaAdapter(...)
Line 75: '# Generate TLEs and compute ISLs on startup' before self.adapter.generate_tles() and self.adapter.calculate_isls()
Line 141: '# --- Fake load assignment ---' is useful as it explains WHY, but line 147: '# --- Bottleneck detection ---' before a loop checking load/cap > 0.8 restates the code
issues.suggestion: Remove restating comments. Keep comments that explain WHY (e.g., '# NOTE: No epoch passed - this is the bug documented in the audit' on line 67 is valuable and should stay).

root_causes: The risk_model.py training functions appear to have been created by copy-paste expansion as new dataset variants were added (legacy, tier1, tier1_v1, design variants), with each copy inheriting the full evaluation boilerplate rather than extracting a shared helper.
The gnn_model.py docstring pattern suggests AI-assisted generation where the model description was produced at multiple levels (module, class, method) without deduplication.
likely_symptoms: formulaic_print_logging is a symptom of missing shared CLI infrastructure rather than a standalone defect
possible_false_positives: engine_pass_through_wrappers — the module is explicitly quarantined/deprecated, so these wrappers may be intentionally kept for backward compatibility during migration. Lower confidence assigned.
